value_chain_position: Financial-management platform layer — operates the dominant US SMB accounting/payroll OS (QuickBooks, 60-85% share), consumer tax filing (TurboTax, dominant in assisted), and the Credit Karma consumer-finance data network. Collectively the operating layer for small-business finance and consumer financial decisions; load-bearing score 5.
gm_trajectory: EXPANDING — company-wide gross margin ~78-81% over FY23-FY25 (FY25 80.8%, scan-flagged 'expanding') on +14% 3-yr revenue CAGR; aggregate GM is a green lie-detector for pricing power, BUT it masks a segment split — TurboTax-DIY 'lost on price' (FY26 Q3) to free/AI competitors while mix-shift into high-ARPU TurboTax Live (+36%) and IES holds the blended line up. Watch for the day the DIY price war shows through to the aggregate.
bull_thesis: Intuit is the clearest toll-taker in US small-business finance: QuickBooks holds ~60-85% of US SMB accounting, TurboTax dominates assisted tax, and Credit Karma is a top consumer-finance data network. The moat reads true where it matters most — the P&L: gross margin ~80.8% and EXPANDING on +15.6% revenue, FCF margin 32-37% (TTM FCF $7.71B), a 7/7 earnings-beat record, Piotroski 8/9, Altman-Z 4.5, near-zero net leverage (net debt only $120M). This is a fortress compounder, not a story stock. The growth has visibly broadened beyond the threatened consumer-tax DIY line: in FY26 Q3 (2026-05-20) the three Big-Bet engines — assisted tax (TurboTax Live +36% rev, now >half of TurboTax), mid-market/IES (online ecosystem +38%, IES contracts +37% QoQ), and the money portfolio (payment volume +30%) — are ALL growing north of 30%, on a $300B TAM at ~6% penetration. The AI-substitution fear is double-hedged: management is embedding OpenAI/ChatGPT and Anthropic/Claude as distribution + 'context' while keeping the 'core' (accuracy, compliance, liability, proprietary data) — '88% of the TurboTax TAM is assisted, where customers buy confidence, not code, and spend 7x more on experts than software.' On valuation, the stock has been crushed (down ~46-55% YTD into this debate, ~$272-300 vs a 2025 peak well over $700), so a best-in-class ~13-14% top-line grower with 32-37% FCF margins is available at ~12.9x P/FCF — a multiple normally reserved for a no-growth utility. Conditions that must hold: (1) mid-market/IES + money keep compounding >30% to out-run the DIY drag; (2) aggregate gross margin stays flat-to-up (no full-franchise tax price war); (3) the securities investigation resolves without a material settlement or pricing-practice injunction.
bear_thesis: The load-bearing leg of the disruptor thesis — that TurboTax's dominance is a durable secular moat — is being disintermediated in real time, and FY26 Q3 is the first quarter it drew blood. Management openly conceded 'I'm constructively dissatisfied... we lost on price' among the most price-sensitive sub-$50K DIY filers (a $5B / 12% slice of the TurboTax TAM), against an industry-wide ~2M-unit (-30bps) filing contraction. The independent web check (dated) is worse than the call: on 2026-06-02 Goldman's Gabriela Borges — the same analyst who probed the AI boundary on the Q2 call — cut INTU to SELL ($276 PT, down from $519), estimating AI models can process a return for ~$0.12 vs TurboTax's ~$162 average revenue/return, with named AI tax upstarts (Prime Meridian, Perplexity Tax, Chime Tax) maturing. There is also an ACTIVE securities-fraud investigation into TurboTax pricing representations during the 2026 season — a real tail liability and governance overhang. Revenue decelerated from +17-18% (FY26 Q1-Q2) to +10% (Q3); latest-quarter EPS YoY is +11%, a sharp step-down from FY25's +31%. The defensive moat-language saturation ('buy confidence not code,' '7x on experts,' 'liability') across the last two calls is itself a tell that management cannot rebut AI substitution on first principles and is leaning on a rehearsed framing. The ~$12B Mailchimp acquisition has quietly become an impairment-in-all-but-name ('run for profitability,' Desktop-style cash cow). Insider open-market reality is net SELLING (2 buys / 6 sells; the scan's 0.87 buy-ratio is grant-inflated). The bear confirmation: a second consecutive quarter of DIY unit loss AND aggregate gross-margin compression — at that point the 'expanding GM' green flag flips and TurboTax re-rates from secular grower to defended-but-shrinking cash cow worth a low-teens multiple.
sop_bull: Favorable segment SoP (peer multiples on FY25 segment revenue; ~277M diluted shares; subtract net debt $120M). GBS $11.08B x 12.0x EV/S (premium SaaS toll-taker, 85% share, IES >30%) = $133B. Consumer/TurboTax $4.87B x 9.0x (durable assisted franchise) = $44B. Credit Karma $2.26B x 6.0x (re-accelerating fintech network) = $14B. Professional Tax $621M x 7.0x = $4B. Sum EV ~$195B; minus net debt $0.12B = equity ~$194.5B; /277M sh = ~$702/share. (Cross-check: a whole-company Buffett/owner-earnings lens with ~$7.7B TTM FCF growing ~15% supports the high-$600s-$700s, consistent with the Graham +58% / IV15 +60% model agreement and the analyst consensus target ~$459 as a more conservative mid-point.)
sop_bear: Adverse segment SoP ASSUMING THE AI-disruption theme pauses 12-18 months and TurboTax-DIY price war bleeds into mix. GBS $11.08B x 7.0x (de-rated, mid-market still grows but multiple compresses) = $78B. Consumer/TurboTax HAIRCUT to $4.4B effective and x 4.5x (assisted defended but DIY units lost, pricing investigation overhang) = $20B. Credit Karma $2.26B x 3.5x (cyclical credit/originations risk) = $8B. Professional Tax $621M x 5.0x = $3B. Sum EV ~$109B; minus net debt $0.12B = equity ~$108.9B; /277M sh = ~$393/share. Hard-downside stress (full tax price-war + multiple compression to a ~14-16x FCF no-growth cash cow on ~$7.7B FCF) breaks toward ~$110-120B equity / low-$400s, with a true tail (settlement + structural DIY erosion) toward the ~$276 Goldman target.
sop_breakdown: Reconciled base-case segment SoP on FY25 revenue, ~277M sh, net debt $120M subtracted. GBS $11.08B x 9.0x EV/S = $100B (the load-bearing engine; mid-market/IES +38% earns a premium-but-not-peak multiple). Consumer/TurboTax $4.87B x 6.5x = $32B (assisted franchise intact, DIY de-rated for the AI/price threat — the single biggest swing factor). Credit Karma $2.26B x 4.75x = $11B (network re-accelerating but credit-cyclical). Professional Tax $621M x 6.0x = $4B. Sum EV ~$147B; minus net debt $0.12B = equity ~$146.5B; /277M sh ~= $529. Blend with the FCF/owner-earnings cross-check (~$7.7B TTM FCF, decelerating-double-digit growth, ~17-19x = ~$130-145B equity, ~$470-525/sh) and a haircut for the unquantified investigation tail lands the reconciled base at ~$470 (mid of $440-$500). This sits ~70% above the ~$277 traded price and well below the bull's ~$702 — the asymmetry is favorable but the left tail (Goldman's $276) is live.
risk_reward: Favorable but not pristine. From ~$277: downside-to-hard-break ~$393 (adverse SoP) is ABOVE the current price — i.e. the market is already pricing close to the bear-SoP, leaving limited incremental downside to the modeled break and a real left-tail only in the true-disruption/settlement scenario (~$276, ~0% further). Upside-to-base-fair ~$470 = +70%; upside-to-bull ~$702 = +153%. Asymmetry roughly +70% base / -0-to-5% to the bear-SoP floor = ~3:1 reward-to-risk on the base case, skewed by the fact that fear is already in the tape. The binding risk is not price level but TIMING: a second DIY-loss quarter + GM compression would reset the floor lower before the mid-market thesis compounds out.
peer_comps_note: theme_map true_competitors: H&R Block, BILL, PCTY, Xero (ASX:XRO), FreshBooks, GOOGL, MSFT. Sanity-check on the multiple: BILL trades ~81-84% gross margin with ~17% core growth; Xero ~$8.9% SMB share vs QuickBooks ~60-85%. INTU's 80.8% GM + 32-37% FCF + 85% category share warrant a premium to BILL/Xero on EV/FCF, but the disruptor LOAD-BEARING fact (TurboTax assisted dominance) is being directly attacked by AI tax upstarts (Prime Meridian/Perplexity Tax/Chime Tax) at a ~$0.12-vs-$162 cost gap per Goldman (2026-06-02) — which is why the base-case Consumer/tax multiple is haircut to ~6.5x and the blended SoP fair value ($470) sits well below the 2025 peak rather than re-rating to it.
consensus_delta: Two-sided and currently OFFSIDE-BEARISH, not offside-bullish. The OLD street assumption being unwound: 'TurboTax dominance is an unassailable secular moat deserving a 30x+ FCF / $700+ multiple' — that is being repriced hard (stock -46-55% YTD; Goldman to SELL $276 on 2026-06-02, PT cut from $519). The NEW consensus error is the opposite extreme: the Goldman bear case extrapolates the ~$0.12-AI-per-return cost gap into terminal TurboTax disintermediation, but the data refutes a clean substitution thesis — 88% of the TurboTax TAM is ASSISTED, where unit and ARPU growth ACCELERATED (TurboTax Live +36% rev, >half of TurboTax; new Live customers +29% ex one-time offers), and the loss was confined to the sub-$50K DIY slice ($5B / 12% of TAM) during an industry-wide ~2M-unit contraction. The street is now mis-pricing the *mix*: it is marking the whole tax franchise to the disrupted DIY low-end while the high-ARPU, expert-attached, liability-bearing assisted core — exactly the part an LLM cannot assume legal liability for — is compounding. The factually-incorrect street assumption is 'AI substitution = TurboTax revenue contraction'; the reality is AI substitution is hollowing the cheapest 12% while the profitable 88% grows. Separately, the analyst-target velocity (-27% vs year-ago PT) and the consensus $459 target confirm the street is mid-capitulation, not complacent.
valley_of_death: Near-term (3-9 month) hazards are real and front-loaded. (1) The FY26 Q4 print (Aug-2026) laps the tax season and will show whether DIY unit loss was one-season or structural; a second consecutive DIY miss + any aggregate gross-margin compression flips the green 'expanding GM' flag and resets the floor toward the low-$400s / Goldman $276. (2) The ACTIVE securities-fraud investigation into TurboTax 2026 pricing representations is an unquantified legal/governance tail that can produce headline risk, a settlement charge, or a pricing-practice injunction at any time — no fixed date, pure overhang. (3) Credit Karma faces harder comps in 2H and is credit-cyclical (originations sensitive to the consumer, which management flagged as 'stretched'). (4) Mailchimp 'run for cash' implies further rightsizing charges. There is NO cash-burn hump or maturity wall — the balance sheet is a fortress ($6.78B cash, net debt $120M, $7.7B FCF) — so the valley is sentiment/legal, not solvency. The stock can stay pinned or drift lower on any of (1)-(2) before mid-market compounding forces the re-rate.
positioning_washout: Largely ALREADY UNDERWAY rather than ahead. INTU is a mega-cap quality/growth name held heavily by passive, growth, and quality-factor funds; the ~46-55% YTD drawdown and the Goldman-to-SELL downgrade have already triggered substantial de-grossing by momentum/quality books (smart-money score 34/100, analyst-target velocity -27%). There is no yield-focused base to force a mechanical dividend-cut exit (no dividend cut at issue; payout is small relative to FCF). The remaining washout risk is GARP/quality funds capitulating if FY26 Q4 confirms structural DIY erosion — a final flush that would be the entry signal. Net: the mechanical selling is ~2/3 done; one more downgrade-or-miss-driven flush is the capitulation to wait for.
forcing_function: No single dated binding catalyst — this is a disruptor durability debate, not a catalyst trade (catalyst_status SOFT_EXTENDED). The hard windows that will re-rate the stock either way: (1) FY26 Q4 earnings, ~late August 2026 — the first post-tax-season print and the August 'sweeping platform/lineup expansion' launch management pre-announced; this is the cleanest read on whether DIY loss is structural and whether mid-market/money sustain >30%. (2) Any development in the TurboTax-pricing securities investigation (settlement, charge, or dismissal) — undated but binary. (3) FY27 guidance at FY26 Q4 — the first guide that must explicitly model AI-tax competition. The forcing function is FY26 Q4 + FY27 guide; the AI-disruption narrative is serially-extended/third-party-driven (Goldman thesis), hence SOFT_EXTENDED, and it must NOT by itself drive the verdict.
moderator_conclusion: This is a high-quality secular toll-taker whose load-bearing leg (TurboTax assisted dominance, theme fintech_rails, value-chain score 5) is under live AI-substitution attack — and the rare case where the VALUE case and the TIMING case diverge sharply, so I score them apart. On theme durability: the secular demand for SMB financial software and assisted tax is real and multi-year, and crucially DIVERSIFIED across ~100M consumers / ~10M businesses / ~1M accountants with NO customer concentration (the risk is category, not customer). The moat lie-detector — gross margin — reads GREEN at the aggregate (80.8% and expanding on +15.6% revenue = genuine pricing power) but I explicitly flag that this aggregate is masking a TurboTax-DIY price war; the single most important forward variable is whether segment-level GM compression bleeds through. Load-bearing position: QuickBooks/TurboTax are hard to route around in the assisted/regulated/liability-bearing core (an LLM cannot assume legal liability for a return), but the cheapest 12% DIY slice CAN be routed around by $0.12-AI/free filers — and that slice is already gone. Reinvestment economics are excellent (32-37% incremental FCF, $300B TAM at ~6%). On valuation: the reconciled base-case SoP fair value is ~$470 (range $440-500), ~70% above the ~$277 tape; the adverse-theme-pause SoP (~$393) sits ABOVE the current price, meaning the market has already discounted close to the bear, and only the true-disruption/settlement tail (~$276, the Goldman target) lies below. That makes this a genuine value opportunity (value_conviction 5: best-in-class quality at ~12.9x P/FCF after a ~50% drawdown, fortress balance sheet, multi-model agreement on Graham/IV15) — but NOT an aggressive-entry timing setup, because the forcing function (FY26 Q4 + FY27 guide, ~Aug-2026) and the active securities investigation create a clear near-term flush risk that I would wait to clear. VERDICT B / conviction 4: WATCHLIST FOR CAPITULATION — accumulate into a FY26 Q4 confirmation of structural DIY loss or an investigation-headline flush, where the GARP base makes its final exit; that is the entry. The catalyst is SOFT_EXTENDED and does not drive this verdict — the durability-vs-disruption read and the already-discounted valley do.
